source: Principia Symbolica Appendix Dual Horizon (frame space, bounded discernibility, orthogonal additivity)
boundary: Mappings certify only the static/algebraic/finite kernel. Manifold, Hilbert-space, PDE, categorical, and asymptotic material is not certified; conditional and partial rows do not promote the full prose statement to a Lean theorem.

entries.note: Modeled as the hypothesis pair (0 < tauE, tauE <= deltaPhi) taken by dual_horizon_signature, rather than as a standalone named Prop.

Proved via the Emergence Domination route (Proof II of the source): bounded reflexive emergence plus the DualHorizonBalance sandwich forces min(G,C) > 0, hence G > 0 and C > 0. The source's alternative Proof I 'from Bounded Observability alone' is not modeled since that assumption is not given with enough precision in the packet.
The full sandwich kappa*m <= deltaPhi <= Lambda*m, sufficiency, necessity, and the tight kappa=Lambda biconditional are all proved unconditionally from the DualHorizonBalance data.

The observer's collapse/refinement map is realized as a resolving function tau -> iota (each token resolves to exactly one frame index), replacing the abstract Proj(Horizon) apparatus with a finite combinatorial model.
Tokens resolving to distinct indices are disjoint, proved directly from the resolving-function model rather than from an operator-orthogonality hypothesis.
Coarse-graining a set of frame indices resolves to exactly the Finset.biUnion of the individual token sets.
Kept as a [0,1]-normalized, binary finitely-additive assignment on Finset tau; the Born-rule token-budget representation formula itself is not modeled.
Binary additivity is extended by induction to any finite pairwise-disjoint indexed family, then combined with the token-resolution model to give additivity of mu over any finite orthogonal family.
Boundedness is derived as a theorem from mu_nonneg plus finite additivity (mu(full) = mu(A) + mu(full\A) >= mu(A)), rather than postulated as a separate axiom.

Conservation (sum over all frame indices = 1) is derived given the added hypothesis that the frame's coarse-graining of every index covers the full admissible token set (tokensOfSet Finset.univ = full); this hypothesis is implicit-by-construction in the source's discernible complete frame but must be stated explicitly here.

Only the finite-dimensional remark is realized: since the frame-index type is a Fintype, summing mu_conservation over Finset.univ already covers every orthogonal family a finite-dimensional Horizon can present. The Gleason-frame-function / normalized-measure identification itself is not separately formalized.
The reflective state space X x H is used directly as the domain/codomain of MemoryAct.step rather than given its own named type.

History-change on every act is proved as a theorem from the strictly increasing order parameter carried by MemoryAct, upgrading the source's postulated axiom to a derived consequence of the monotone-order model.
Every act incurs strictly positive cost, direct from the MemoryAct.cost_pos field.

The order parameter accumulates by at least n over n steps, hence the history never returns to an earlier value along any nontrivial path -- the discrete/finite kernel of the induced directed order and arrow of time.
V(C) = (1/2)(C - 1/C)^2 kept exactly; nonnegativity is unconditional, the zero-locus characterization (V(C)=0 iff C=1) is proved for C > 0.

Reframed algebraically from the source's asymptotic-ratio condition to the fixed-point inequality theta >= 1 + 1/theta (the same inequality theorem:appC_phi_minimal_curvature_parameter states verbatim for the curvature parameter) -- an explicit honesty gap against the source's limit-of-sequence phrasing.
phi is the least value satisfying the algebraic reframing of Sustainable; not a statement about limits of sequences of ratios.
Kept as a pair-valued map (x,y) |-> (x+y,x) rather than a 2x2 matrix.
Same formula theta + 1/theta as definition:appC_symbolic_curvature_function, stated once and reused for both anchors.
Proved among Sustainable rates (the algebraic reframing), not among all sustainable-in-the-source's-asymptotic-sense rates.

Only the contraction clause (iii) is modeled, as a global hypothesis rather than one restricted to an unspecified neighborhood U. The invariance clause (i) and the curvature inner-product clause (ii) are dropped -- no operator K_t is modeled in this pass.
Given the PhiStableRegion's global contraction hypothesis, distance to M shrinks geometrically: infDist(x_n, M) <= q^n * infDist(x_0, M).
The two-step closure hypothesis s(n+2)=s(n+1)+s(n) is taken directly as a hypothesis of Gop_step rather than given its own named Prop.
Gop advances consecutive terms of any two-step-closure sequence: Gop(s(n+1),s(n)) = (s(n+2),s(n+1)).
Reframed from a Perron-Frobenius/matrix-norm spectral-radius argument to a direct eigenvector exhibition: Gop(phi,1) = (phi*phi,phi) = phi . (phi,1).
Identical formula to definition:appC_complexity_entropy_tradeof (theta + 1/theta), stated once as kappa and reused for both anchors rather than duplicated.

The same theorem as theorem:appC_phi_minimized_entropy_per_complexity, stated twice in the source under two names; both are discharged by kappa_min_at_phi (and its minimality precondition by sustainable_ge_phi), restricted to the algebraic Sustainable domain theta >= 1 + 1/theta given verbatim by this anchor.
The stability condition |d/dtheta(1+1/theta)| < 1 at theta=phi is realized directly as the closed-form inequality 1/(phi*phi) < 1, without modeling a general derivative operator.
1/(phi*phi) < 1, proved from phi > 1.
Among positive reals, phi is the unique fixed point of lam = 1 + 1/lam, proved via the same factoring argument as sustainable_ge_phi plus uniqueness of the positive root of lam^2-lam-1=0.
Only the pointwise real identity x = max(x,0)-max(-x,0) underlying the divergence-theorem remark is modeled; the observer measure and manifold integrals G_Obs, C_Obs themselves are not.
Witnessed only for the canonical positive-initial-data instance C_n=fib(n+1) (C_0=C_1=1); generalizing to arbitrary C_0,C_1>0 is not attempted.
Frame modeled as a Finset rather than a Hilbert-space metric ball.
Finset.card of an intersection stands in for dim(span(...)); genuine linear-algebra dimension is not modeled.
The scalar growth-rate content of rho(G)=phi is witnessed by the same golden-ratio limit; the operator G and its spectral radius/operator norm are not modeled.
Exact matrix-power identity for n>=1, reindexed n|->n+1 to avoid Nat subtraction at the excluded n=0 case.
theta as forward-drift rate, 1/theta as curvature penalty, kept as plain reals rather than a manifold dynamical system.
AM-GM lower bound on kappa(theta)=theta+1/theta plus its equality trichotomy at theta=1.
Only clause (2), observer-bounded distortion, is modeled; ordinal preservation, operator semi-conjugacy, and invariant preservation are not.

Distortion-composition bound covers the quantitative 'preserved up to observer resolution' content under composition; the golden-ratio tail clause is covered by the shared Fibonacci-ratio theorem. Ordinal/operator-recurrence invariant preservation itself is not modeled.
The Born coherence form satisfies unitary covariance; forward direction of the Born rule.
The measure family is unitary-covariant - same kernel as PS-C2.
The Born form is phase-invariant (ray invariance).
The Born form calibrates: a pure state answers its own question with coherence 1.
The rank-one Born value taken as the coherence functional, computed on the qubit and bounded by 1; Gleason-type uniqueness (axioms force this form in d>=3) stays a counsel-permanent open.
The qubit Born value equals the squared amplitude, computed on C^2.
The mixed-state coherence is affine in the mixing weights, matching tr(rho Pi_a).

Typed noncontextuality axiom: a budget satisfying NoncontextualAt gives equal values for the same question across frames. Countermodel confirms unit-interval boundedness does not derive frame independence, matching the source declaration that PS-C3-prime is posited.

Typed resolution axiom excludes simultaneous unit coherence for separated orthogonal questions. Countermodel confirms ordinary [0,1] boundedness does not derive PS-C5; it remains an explicit physical axiom.

Exact operational kernel under the stated one-step-memory assumption: a current-only scalar update cannot distinguish histories sharing the current value and cannot represent the rule next=previous; the two-coordinate state (current, previous) represents every one-step recurrence and shifts memory exactly.

Analytic kernel: bounded continuous flows into a complete normed target are complete in the uniform ambient metric, and a shared Lipschitz bound—including the printed C1*delta bound—passes to pointwise limits. A countermodel shows pointwise convergence without a common bound is insufficient. The exact custom Lipschitz norm and bounded-operator specialization are not reconstructed.
